label: Occupancy rate
formula: Total resident days / total bed days available
definition: The share of available bed days filled by residents during the cost-reporting period.
strategic_use: Core utilization metric for demand, capacity use, referral strength, and excess capacity.
high_value_interpretation: Higher occupancy generally suggests stronger demand or better use of capacity, though very high occupancy can reduce flexibility.
low_value_interpretation: Lower occupancy may suggest referral leakage, market competition, reputation issues, or excess capacity.
trend_interpretation: A declining trend can signal weakening market position; improvement can suggest better referral capture or demand recovery.
caveats: Interpret occupancy with payer mix, margin, staffing, and quality; occupancy alone does not prove financial strength.

label: Average daily census
formula: Total resident days / report days used for rates
definition: Estimated average resident census during the cost-reporting period.
strategic_use: Gives an intuitive size and volume measure that complements beds and occupancy.
high_value_interpretation: Higher ADC usually indicates larger operating scale or stronger utilization.
low_value_interpretation: Lower ADC may indicate small scale, excess capacity, or weak demand.
trend_interpretation: A declining ADC trend can explain worsening cost per day or margin pressure.
caveats: Uses cost-report period days; partial or irregular reports should be reviewed with data-quality flags.

label: Medicare day share
definition: The share of total resident days paid by Medicare.
strategic_use: Proxy for skilled/post-acute orientation and hospital referral capture.
high_value_interpretation: Higher share may reflect stronger post-acute volume, skilled care, or Medicare exposure.
low_value_interpretation: Lower share may indicate long-stay/custodial orientation or weaker skilled referral capture.
trend_interpretation: Declining share may indicate loss of post-acute referrals or shift toward long-stay utilization.
caveats: Not a direct case-mix measure; interpret later with PBJ staffing, quality, hospital referrals, and Medicare Advantage context.

label: Medicaid day share
definition: The share of total resident days paid by Medicaid.
strategic_use: Assesses long-stay payer dependence and exposure to state Medicaid reimbursement policy.
high_value_interpretation: Higher share often reflects a long-stay nursing facility model and may increase reimbursement adequacy risk.
low_value_interpretation: Lower share may reflect more post-acute, private-pay, or other-payer business mix.
trend_interpretation: Rising share can indicate increasing dependence on long-stay Medicaid volume.
caveats: High Medicaid share is not automatically negative; interpret with state rates, costs, occupancy, and margin.

label: Other/private day share
definition: The share of resident days not classified as Medicare or Medicaid.
strategic_use: Broad proxy for private pay, managed care, commercial, or other payer activity.
high_value_interpretation: Higher share may reflect payer diversity or private/managed-care activity.
low_value_interpretation: Lower share may indicate heavier reliance on Medicare and Medicaid programs.
trend_interpretation: Changing trend may suggest shifts in payer contracts, resident mix, or market dynamics.
caveats: This is a broad residual category and should not be treated as a clean private-pay measure without payer detail.

label: Net patient revenue per resident day
formula: Net patient revenue / total resident days
definition: Net patient care revenue divided by total resident days.
strategic_use: Evaluates revenue yield, reimbursement adequacy, payer mix, and payment performance.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may reflect stronger skilled mix, reimbursement, acuity, or payer contracts.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may reflect Medicaid dependence, weak contracts, lower acuity, or limited skilled volume.
trend_interpretation: Compare trend with expense per day to identify margin compression or improvement.
caveats: Do not interpret revenue per day without payer mix and case-mix context.

label: Operating expense per resident day
definition: Total operating expense divided by total resident days.
strategic_use: Assesses cost intensity, operating efficiency, labor pressure, and sustainability.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may reflect staffing costs, contract labor, acuity, overhead, capital, or inefficiency.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may reflect efficiency, lower labor costs, lower acuity, or possible underinvestment.
trend_interpretation: If expenses grow faster than revenue per day, margin pressure may increase.
caveats: Lower expense is not always better; interpret with staffing, quality, acuity, and occupancy.

label: Revenue-expense spread per resident day
formula: Net patient revenue per day - operating expense per day
definition: Difference between daily net patient revenue and daily operating expense.
strategic_use: Makes margin pressure visible in dollar-per-day terms.
high_value_interpretation: Positive spread suggests daily patient revenue exceeds operating expense.
low_value_interpretation: Negative spread suggests daily expenses exceed patient revenue.
trend_interpretation: Trend reveals whether financial pressure is improving or worsening before looking at ratios.
caveats: This is a simplified operating spread and should be reconciled with formal margin fields.

label: Patient service margin
formula: Net income from service to patients / net patient revenue
definition: Net income from patient care services as a share of net patient revenue.
strategic_use: Assesses whether patient care operations are financially sustainable.
high_value_interpretation: Positive margin suggests care delivery revenue is covering related operating costs.
low_value_interpretation: Negative margin suggests patient care operations are not covering costs.
trend_interpretation: Worsening trend may indicate revenue pressure, cost growth, payer-mix deterioration, or staffing pressure.
caveats: Does not explain cause; use with revenue/day, expense/day, payer mix, and labor metrics.

label: Total margin
definition: Overall net income as a share of net patient revenue.
strategic_use: Evaluates total financial performance after other income effects.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value indicates stronger overall surplus relative to care revenue.
low_value_interpretation: Lower or negative value indicates weaker overall financial performance.
trend_interpretation: Trend reveals whether overall profitability is improving or deteriorating.
caveats: Can be influenced by accounting, ownership structure, related parties, and other income.

label: Net income per resident day
definition: Net income normalized by resident days.
strategic_use: Shows financial result in an operating unit that is easy to compare across facilities.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value indicates more surplus per resident day.
low_value_interpretation: Lower or negative value indicates financial pressure per resident day.
trend_interpretation: Trend can reveal improvement/deterioration even when percentages are noisy.
caveats: Affected by accounting and non-patient income; pair with patient service margin.

label: Salary cost per resident day
definition: Adjusted salary expense divided by total resident days.
strategic_use: Assesses direct labor cost intensity and staffing-cost pressure.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may indicate higher staffing levels, wages, acuity, or labor-market pressure.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may reflect efficiency, lower wage markets, lower staffing intensity, or possible underinvestment.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend may indicate wage inflation, staffing changes, or acuity changes.
caveats: Cost-report salary data does not directly measure staffing hours or quality; PBJ should be added later.

label: Contract labor per resident day
definition: Contract labor expense divided by total resident days.
strategic_use: Assesses agency or contracted labor cost pressure per resident day.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may signal staffing shortages, agency reliance, or temporary labor-market disruption.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may indicate limited contract-labor reliance or stronger employee staffing stability.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend may be an early signal of staffing stress or wage pressure.
caveats: Some contract labor can be normal or temporary; pair with PBJ and local labor-market context.

label: Contract labor ratio
definition: Contract labor as a share of combined adjusted salaries and contract labor.
strategic_use: Assesses workforce instability and reliance on agency/contracted staffing.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may indicate staffing shortages, retention problems, agency dependence, or labor-market stress.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may indicate more stable employed staffing or less agency dependence.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend is often a warning sign for operating cost and staffing stability.
caveats: Financial proxy only; PBJ staffing-hours data should be added for stronger operations analysis.

label: Total labor cost per resident day
formula: (Adjusted salaries + wage-related costs + contract labor) / total resident days
definition: Combined reported labor, wage-related, and contract labor cost per resident day.
strategic_use: Broader labor-cost pressure measure than salaries alone.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may reflect wage pressure, benefits burden, contract labor, acuity, or staffing intensity.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may reflect efficiency, lower wages, or possible underinvestment.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend may signal labor inflation or increasing agency/benefit costs.
caveats: Uses public-use-file labor fields; detailed direct-care labor requires raw HCRIS.

label: Labor cost share of operating expense
formula: (Adjusted salaries + wage-related costs + contract labor) / operating expense
definition: Share of operating expense attributable to broad labor-cost fields.
strategic_use: Shows whether cost pressure is primarily labor-driven.
high_value_interpretation: Higher share suggests labor is a major cost driver.
low_value_interpretation: Lower share suggests other costs, overhead, or capital may be more important.
trend_interpretation: Trend helps identify whether labor cost pressure is growing.
caveats: Broad proxy; public-use-file labor categories may not perfectly isolate direct care labor.

label: Wage-related cost ratio
definition: Wage-related costs as a share of adjusted salaries.
strategic_use: Proxy for benefits, payroll taxes, and other wage-related burden.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value suggests heavier benefit/payroll burden or accounting allocation differences.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value suggests lower wage-related burden or different reporting practices.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend can contribute to labor cost pressure even without salary growth.
caveats: Should be reviewed with salary and contract labor metrics.

label: Days cash on hand
definition: Estimated days the facility could cover operating expenses using reported cash.
strategic_use: Assesses liquidity cushion and short-term financial resilience.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value suggests stronger liquidity cushion.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may indicate financial fragility or limited cash reserves.
trend_interpretation: Declining trend can signal weakening liquidity even before margins deteriorate.
caveats: Balance-sheet values can be affected by timing, ownership, related parties, and accounting practices.

label: Current ratio
definition: Current assets divided by current liabilities.
strategic_use: Evaluates short-term ability to cover obligations due within a year.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value generally suggests stronger short-term liquidity.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may signal near-term financial pressure or limited working capital.
trend_interpretation: Declining trend can indicate growing liquidity risk.
caveats: Interpret with days cash, payables, accounts receivable, and ownership context.

label: Days accounts receivable
formula: Accounts receivable / (net patient revenue / report days)
definition: Estimated days of net patient revenue tied up in accounts receivable.
strategic_use: Assesses billing, collection timing, and working-capital pressure.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may indicate slower collections, payer delays, or billing issues.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may suggest faster collections or less receivables-intensive payer mix.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend can increase cash pressure even when reported revenue is stable.
caveats: Receivables reflect timing and accounting practices; interpret with payer mix and revenue cycle context.

label: Debt to assets
definition: Total liabilities as a share of total assets.
strategic_use: Assesses leverage, solvency, and balance-sheet risk.
high_value_interpretation: Higher value may indicate greater leverage and less financial flexibility.
low_value_interpretation: Lower value may indicate less leverage or stronger asset cushion.
trend_interpretation: Rising trend can indicate increasing balance-sheet risk, especially with negative margins.
caveats: Facility balance sheets can be affected by ownership structure and related-party arrangements.
